title: Back out the button 5 collider-suppression experiment
summary: 0.6.34 removes the 0.6.33 collider-suppression layer after operator testing showed it only brought the visible pointers back without unlocking grab-nav. The live lane returns to the simpler 0.6.32 button 5 behavior.
changes: Remove the temporary suppression of player-owned screen and control interaction colliders while button 5 is held.
Remove the hosted-surface helper that only existed to support that suppression path.
Keep the simpler 0.6.32 rule: button 5 disables player gaze and focus behavior while held.
reasoning: The 0.6.33 interaction-layer experiment did not unlock grab-nav; it only made the pointers or lasers visible.
Keeping dead-end complexity in the input seam would make later navigation polish harder to reason about.
At this point, the remaining possible route is VaM-global UI-hit/worldUI behavior, which is more invasive than this lane should safely become.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: The player should behave like 0.6.32 again for button 5, without the extra collider toggling.
The visible-pointer side effect from 0.6.33 should be gone.
knownIssues: No further safe player-owned route is currently identified for the remaining grab-nav lock while looking at the screen.
This slice does not add the queued post-navigation deadband, slideshow work, A-B policy changes, volume gestures, or spatial audio.
